mandates.mandate: Every completed step writes a checkpoint. No exceptions.
SHA256 verification is the integrity guarantee for resume.
Checkpoints survive context resets. They are the persistence layer.
On resume, the user must acknowledge any out-of-band file modifications.

step.title: Write checkpoint (after-step-complete)
step.action: Receive: workflow_id, run_id, current_step_index, files_touched updates, status.
For each new file in files_touched: compute SHA256 hash.

step.title: Verify file integrity on resume
step.action: For each files_touched entry: compute current SHA256, compare to stored hash.
All match: return VERIFIED, report "Resuming from step N".
Mismatch: list changed files with path, expected SHA256, actual SHA256. Offer three options:
[p] Proceed — accept out-of-band changes, resume from next step.
[s] Start fresh — archive checkpoint, restart workflow from step 1.
[r] Review — show diff for each changed file, let user decide per-file.
Wait for user response. Do not auto-proceed.
